raw_text:
TITLE: Peanut Butter Cookies

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 cups all purpose flour
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 cup unsalted butter, softened
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 large egg
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 1 cup creamy peanut butter
- 2 cups semisweet chocolate chips

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats. Set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour and baking soda. Set aside.
3. In another large mixing bowl, beat the butter until creamy using an electric mixer on medium speed. Add the granulated sugar and continue beating for 1-2 minutes or until well combined.
4. Beat in the egg and vanilla extract until fully incorporated. Scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed to ensure even mixing.
5. Reduce the mixer speed to low, then add the peanut butter and beat just until combined. Be careful not to overmix or incorporate too much air into the dough.
6. Gradually stir in the flour mixture until a soft dough forms. Fold in the chocolate chips using a spatula or wooden spoon.
7. Using a cookie scoop, portion out 12-15 balls of dough onto each prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart to allow for spreading during baking.
8. Bake for 9-10 minutes or until the edges are lightly golden and the centers appear set. Remove from oven and let cool on the baking sheets for a few minutes before transferring to wire racks to cool completely. Enjoy!

TEMPERATURE: Not specified in image provided.

structured.title: Peanut Butter Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour and baking soda. Set aside.
In another large mixing bowl, beat the butter until creamy using an electric mixer on medium speed. Add the granulated sugar and continue beating for 1-2 minutes or until well combined.
Beat in the egg and vanilla extract until fully incorporated. Scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed to ensure even mixing.
Reduce the mixer speed to low, then add the peanut butter and beat just until combined. Be careful not to overmix or incorporate too much air into the dough.
Gradually stir in the flour mixture until a soft dough forms. Fold in the chocolate chips using a spatula or wooden spoon.
Using a cookie scoop, portion out 12-15 balls of dough onto each prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart to allow for spreading during baking.

Bake for 9-10 minutes or until the edges are lightly golden and the centers appear set. Remove from oven and let cool on the baking sheets for a few minutes before transferring to wire racks to cool completely. Enjoy!
